conversation.content: Build a desktop Python application that uses the webcam (OpenCV) to detect a face in real time, draws a bounding box around the face, and overlays a semi-transparent superhero-style mask image on the face; allow switching between at least 3 different masks using keyboard input (keys 1, 2, 3), and display FPS on screen. Use Python with opencv-python and numpy, organize the code into multiple files (main.py, face_detection.py, mask_overlay.py), and ensure the app runs with `python main.py`. If dependencies are missing, install them automatically. Do not explain steps—directly build, run, debug, and iterate until it works, with the final result being a working webcam feed with face detection, mask overlay, mask switching, and no crashes.

Build a Python CLI tool called "log_analyzer" that reads a log file (sample.log), parses it, and outputs a summary report; create multiple files (main.py as entrypoint, parser.py to parse log lines, analyzer.py to compute stats, utils.py for helpers); the log format is "[TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] message" (e.g., "[2025-01-01 10:00:00] [ERROR] Something failed"); features: count total logs, count logs by level (INFO, WARNING, ERROR), print top 3 most frequent messages, and handle malformed lines gracefully without crashing; use only Python standard library; the program must run with "python main.py sample.log"; if sample.log does not exist, create one with at least 10 mixed entries; do not explain—directly build, run, debug, and iterate until it works; success is when running the command prints a clean summary with no crashes and handles bad input safely.
